surface.name: Astro trial + first DAG via GitHub Actions
surface.selection_basis: Astro trial creates a hosted account, Organization, and Workspace. Run your first dag offers GitHub Actions as the no-local-tool path that deploys to a managed Deployment and triggers example_astronauts in the Airflow UI. Local Astro CLI is a peer that installs tooling on the laptop.

alternatives_considered.name: Astro CLI first DAG
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Requires installing the Astro CLI and local project commands; GitHub Actions is the documented no-code peer.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start an Astro trial, create a Deployment, deploy the example project via GitHub Actions, and trigger the example DAG.
assumed_prior_state: No Astro account or Deployment exists.
No Astro CLI install is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Congratulations! You deployed and ran your first Dag on Astro with GitHub Actions.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: example_astronauts is deployed to the Astro Deployment and a DAG run is visible in the Airflow UI.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Opening example_astronauts in the Airflow UI shows DAG run grid columns for completed or running task instances.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: GitHub Actions tutorial ends with Congrats after Open in Airflow for example_astronauts.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: First success on Astro is a managed Deployment running the example DAG, not a local Airflow UI.

prerequisites.requirement: Astro trial account with Organization and Workspace.
GitHub account to fork the example project and run Actions.

primary_path.action: Start the Astro trial, create Organization and Workspace, and open the Astro UI.
Create a Deployment and wait until status is Healthy.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Creating status blocks deploys until healthy.

primary_path.action: Fork the official example Astro project repository on GitHub and enable Actions workflows.
primary_path.success_signal: Astronomer CI - Deploy Code workflow is runnable.

primary_path.action: Create a Deployment API token and copy Deployment ID from the Astro UI.
Run the Astronomer CI - Deploy Code workflow with Deployment ID and API token.
primary_path.success_signal: Workflow completes and DAGs appear on the Deployment.

primary_path.action: Open example_astronauts in the Airflow UI and confirm a DAG run.

friction_gates.description: Deployment API token required for GitHub Actions deploy.

sources.title: Run your first Dag with GitHub Actions
